cards.title: Steps of a Claim
cards.content:
1. Intimation: Immediate notice of loss to the insurer.
2. Investigation: Surveyor appointed to assess the damage.
3. Assessment: Calculating the actual loss (applying depreciation/deductibles).
4. Settlement: Payment made after signing the Discharge Voucher.

items.text: Independent professionals licensed by IRDAI. Mandatory for Motor/Fire claims above Γé╣50,000.
The damaged property. Once the claim is settled on "Total Loss" basis, salvage belongs to the Insurer.
Subrogation allows the insurer to recover the paid amount from the third party who caused the loss.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Notice of Loss must be given immediately. Any delay can prejudice the insurer's ability to investigate.
Arbitration: Only used to resolve disputes regarding the QUANTUM (Amount) of claim, NOT liability.
Ex-gratia Payment: Made out of compassion or commercial goodwill when there is no strict legal liability to pay.
Discharge Voucher: Signing this means the insured accepts the payment as Full and Final settlement.
Partial Loss vs Total Loss: If repair cost exceeds 75% of IDV in motor insurance, it is treated as a Constructive Total Loss (CTL).
